solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public List<Integer> lexicalOrder(int n) {
        List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
        int num = 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            result.add(num);
            if (num * 10 <= n) {
                num *= 10;
            } else {
                while (num % 10 == 9 || num == n) {
                    num /= 10;
                }
                num += 1;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a depth-first approach to generate numbers in lexicographical order. It starts from 1 and tries to multiply by 10 to go deeper in the lexicographical sequence. If it exceeds n, it backtracks by dividing by 10 until it can increment the number. This approach ensures O(n) time and O(1) space.
